scenes.audio_text: Everyone worships busy people like they're important. I think they're just afraid of being alone with their own thoughts.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel stands in a bright, minimalist kitchen with morning light streaming through a window. Medium close-up, locked-off camera, natural lighting. He speaks directly to the lens with a slight smirk. Photo-real, documentary style.

scenes.audio_text: I used to fill every hour with meetings, calls, side projects. Looked productive. Felt empty.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel walks down a quiet city street at golden hour, wearing the same dark polo. Over-the-shoulder shot, slow tracking forward, warm sunlight filtering through trees. Candid, real-life feel.

scenes.audio_text: Then I spent a week doing nothing. No phone, no laptop. Just sat in a park.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel sits on a wooden park bench under a large oak tree, afternoon sunlight dappling his face. Medium shot, slow push-in, natural lighting. He looks relaxed, thoughtful. Photo-real.

scenes.audio_text: By day three, my brain started surfacing ideas I'd buried for years. That one creative spark paid more than a month of grinding.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel stands at a street market, holding a coffee cup, looking off-camera thoughtfully. Medium close-up, shallow depth of field, warm afternoon light. Documentary style.

scenes.audio_text: Busy is a shield. Drop it. See what's underneath.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel back in the same kitchen as scene 1, now leaning against the counter, arms crossed. Medium close-up, locked-off, natural morning light. He delivers the line with a direct, confident gaze.
